title: Average inflation rate in the United States from 1980 to 2031
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The average inflation rate in the U.S. from 1980 to 2026 was about 3.1% annually. This figure is based on historical Consumer Price Index data.

# U.S. Inflation Rates by Year: Historical Data (1914–2026). U.S. inflation rates by year show how consumer prices have changed annually based on the Consumer Price Index (CPI) published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS - Consumer Price Index")). The table below provides historical U.S. inflation rate data from 1914 through 2026, allowing readers to compare yearly inflation levels over more than a century. The CPI is released monthly by the BLS and serves as the primary measure of inflation in the United States. The latest CPI data, covering inflation through March 2026, was released on April 10. The next CPI report is scheduled for May 12 and will include inflation data through April 2026. For charts and tables focused on current U.S. inflation rates, see our page covering the latest inflation data. To estimate the cumulative inflation rate between specific dates, use the U.S. Inflation Calculator. ### U.S. Inflation Rate by Year (1914–2026) — Historical Data.

# US Inflation Rate by Year. | Mar 1, 2026 | 3.26% |. Inflation rate based on 12-month change in CPI. Sources:* US Bureau of Labor Statistics for current US inflation rate.* Robert Shiller and his book Irrational Exuberance. for historic US inflation rates.. US Bureau of Labor Statistics.
